docs.text: Welcome _I have many interests; devops/tech/cloud/kubernetes, music, bass guitar, composition and performance. I have run my own consulting company, Creative Possibilities since 2000. Original Code Site, have a site for scripts and metastore for tech, music, devops projects. Resume Music YouTube Channel Twitter Feed Creative Possibilities Inc. LinkedIn Credly - Certifications GitHub Repos Demos and Experiments Infrastructure as Code: Experimenting with Terraform and Pulumi Cloud Certification: AWS, Azure, GCP Creating a simple easy to update website with Git and Mkdocs Stand up a docker container for tools using podman _I have included this mkDocs site to maintain my personal website easier than other methods (e.g. wordpress). I figured it's also a cool way to learn and create on things I dig, like github.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Big Honking DevOps Page of Love DevOps Resources DevOpsDays Learning Platforms DevOps Notes and Resources Germinated as conversations around Agile System Admin practices: @littleidea, @patrickdebois MeetUp organized by Patrick Debois in Ghent, Belgium 2009 Decade Anniversary John Willis aka @Botchagalupe the meta-gist motherlode of DevOps Goodness CALMS: C ulture, A utomation, L ean, M easurement, S haring (John Willis/Damon Edwardsi for moree see DevOps Cafe) What is the role of Site Reliability Engineer? SRE Book and SRE Workbook . How SRE relates to DevOps DORA DevOps Data and Metrics singularity Dr. Nicole Forsgren @nicolefv Accelerate (deserves its own entry with DevOps Handbooks) Jez Humble Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (also DORA and Accelerate) Mark Burgess CFEngine, Promise Theory and how the field of physics informs IT. Burgess pretty much invented modern config management space. By extension heavily influenced Adam Jacob @OPsCode/Chef Config Management Prime-Movers : Luke Kaines (Puppet) and Adam Jacob (Chef) Michael DeHann (Ansible) Mitchell Hashimoto (Terraform) Jeff Snover (PowerShell) Gene Kim (Visible Ops, ITRevolution, DOES) @realgenekim When IT Fails DevOps Enterprise Summits Netflix Culture Deck and Patty McCord TED TALK Adrian Cockroft (Sun/Netflix/AWS) Chaos Engineering and a ton more. Check his talks and dig his book recommendations Podcasts: Cloud Cafe, DevOps Cafe, Arrested Devops, The Ship Show, Software Defined Talk DevOps Readiness Assessment DORA DevOpsDays : Conferences arguably were the structures that germanated the movement's accelerated adoption and cloud. The conferences help ignite the DevOps cultural movement/moment. DevSecOps: James Wickett, Rudgged DevOps Justin Cormack (IAmTheCalvary) Simon Wardley: Wardley Mapping as a strategic tool. It's like he can tell the future and often does. The Emergence of Serverless Twitter handles for some cool folks in DevOps and SRE here Deming, TPS/Ohno and Lean Dr Edwards Deming The tremendous influence of Dr. Edwards Deming. See Profound site Podcast the SoPK Toyota Production System, Taiichi Ohno, Lean Manufacturing Goldratt the Theory of Constraints (TOC). Also see The Goal and The Phoenix Project (Gene Kim) Seminal Videos John Allspaw and Paul Hammond at Flickr 10+ deploys a day (Velocity 2009) Ben Rockwood: The DevOps Transformation Andrew Clay Shafer There is No Talent Shortage Alice GoldFuss MN DoD 2019 The Containe Operator Manual Kelly Shortridge and Dr. Nicole Forsgren Controlled Chaos: The Inevitable Marriage of DevOps & Security Seminal Texts The Lean Startup Steven Blank's book The Four Steps to Ephiphany heavily influenced Eric Reis as a student at Stanford.
docs.title: DevOps

How SRE relates to DevOps DORA DevOps Data and Metrics singularity Dr. Nicole Forsgren @nicolefv Accelerate (deserves its own entry with DevOps Handbooks) Jez Humble Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (also DORA and Accelerate) Mark Burgess CFEngine, Promise Theory and how the field of physics informs IT. Burgess pretty much invented modern config management space. By extension heavily influenced Adam Jacob @OPsCode/Chef Config Management Prime-Movers : Luke Kaines (Puppet) and Adam Jacob (Chef) Michael DeHann (Ansible) Mitchell Hashimoto (Terraform) Jeff Snover (PowerShell) Gene Kim (Visible Ops, ITRevolution, DOES) @realgenekim When IT Fails DevOps Enterprise Summits Netflix Culture Deck and Patty McCord TED TALK Adrian Cockroft (Sun/Netflix/AWS) Chaos Engineering and a ton more. Check his talks and dig his book recommendations Podcasts: Cloud Cafe, DevOps Cafe, Arrested Devops, The Ship Show, Software Defined Talk DevOps Readiness Assessment DORA DevOpsDays : Conferences arguably were the structures that germanated the movement's accelerated adoption and cloud. The conferences help ignite the DevOps cultural movement/moment. DevSecOps: James Wickett, Rudgged DevOps Justin Cormack (IAmTheCalvary) Simon Wardley: Wardley Mapping as a strategic tool. It's like he can tell the future and often does. The Emergence of Serverless Twitter handles for some cool folks in DevOps and SRE here

docs.text: Overview AWS SAA Certified Solutions Architect - Associate Lifelong learner inspired by DevOps culture, principles, practices and tools Solid communicator cross-functional team collaborator & Individual Contributor Opensource advocate, Ops and Infra-nerd, Architect and Monitoring savant Passions: Kubernetes, Cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP), Automation, Infrastructure as Code, DevOps Toolchains, Toil reduction, Music Previous Lives: Unix Sysadmin, DBA, Systems/Technical Architect, Monitoring and CRM/ERP SME, Solutions Architect, Ops specialist Cloud AWS: Amazon Web Services Certified Solutions Architect Azure : Familiar with ACS, AKS, ARM templates, Bicep, Azure CAF GCP: Passionate about building infrastructures in GCP Containers, Kubernetes and Automation Containerization/Orchestration: Docker, Kubernetes and Nomad IaC and CI/CD: Terraform, CloudFormation, ARM templates, Ansible, Git Monitoring: Prometheus 2.x, Certified Zabbix Specialist/Professional, CloudWatch, Legacy stacks Tracing and Observability: BPF/bpftrace, tcpdump, Jaeger, DTrace Web: Apache, Nginx, HAProxy, ALB/ELB, Auto Scaling (k8s and AWS) DevOps DevOps evangelist Operating Systems & Databases Linux: RHEL6-8.x/CentOS, Ubuntu 20, Alpine 3.15, SUSE 9-11, zLinux, Slackware and other flavors DBA/RDBMS: RDS, MariaDB (Multi-Primary), MySQL, DB2, Oracle, MS SQL Server, BI and DW solutions Storage: S3, GCS, Azure Storage, EBS, EFS, GPD, CSIs, NetApp, NFS, AFS, DFS, GPFS Data Stores/NoSQL: MongoDB, DynamoDB, etcd Reporting: Grafana, Graphite, Cognos, BIRT, Business Objects, SSRS, Actuate Network: Tool SME (RMON and SNMP) Enterprise: ERP (SAP Basis 4.x), CRM (Siebel 7.04) Legacy OSes: Solaris, AIX (certified ACP), HP/UX, DynaxPtx LOL Scripting and Languages Scripting: BASH, PERL, PowerShell, Korn Languages: Python, PERL, C, Golang DSL: YAML, JSON APIs: AWS API Gateway, REST, k8s IDE/ISE: PyCharm, VSCode, Cloud9, Eclipse, MS PoSh, vi Compilers: gcc, xlC, cc, c++, make, gmake
